metric_value: Leading supplier to forward-thinking data warehouse users
notes: Aberdeen describes Prism as 'leading supplier to forward-thinking data warehouse users'

metric_value: Scalability requires managing metadata changes not just database size growth
notes: Aberdeen redefines DW scalability to include metadata and architectural change management — not just storage growth

metric_value: Business-driven demand: modify business rules alter data sources add technologies
notes: Enterprise DW evolution is driven by business insights generating demand for change

metric_value: Data mart proliferation could choke enterprises relying on non-scalable warehousing techniques
notes: Aberdeen warning: rapid data mart proliferation creates metadata redundancy risk without proper management

metric_value: Robust metadata and tool-driven proficiency is the key to managing new architectures
notes: Aberdeen central thesis: metadata management is THE key to data warehouse architectural integrity

metric_value: Common GUI across all Prism applications; integrates design construction maintenance
notes: Aberdeen endorses Prism Warehouse Executive as delivering on Prism's charter of integrated DW lifecycle management

metric_value: Metadata facility for both business and technical users; CDIF exchange with CASE tools
notes: Prism Warehouse Directory provides metadata navigation for developers and business users

metric_value: Java and HTML views of metadata for browser users; $2500 for 5 concurrent seats
notes: Q1 1997 release; early web-based BI/metadata access; $2500 for 5 seats pricing

metric_value: Business users build their own web-based views of desired data independently of IS
notes: Precursor to modern self-service BI; business users building personal marts without IS involvement

metric_value: Developers reuse particular mappings tables and user exits within mappings
notes: New feature enabling reuse of warehouse components; precursor to modern ETL template libraries

metric_value: 400 customers for data migration technology and consulting services
notes: From Ardent acquisition announcement (ESJ Jan 1999): Prism had 400 customers at time of acquisition

metric_value: 250 data warehouse implementation and consulting engagements inform Iterations methodology
notes: Iterations methodology based on 250 real DW engagements — significant empirical foundation for 1997

metric_value: DB2/MVS, DB2/400, Enscribe, IDMS, IMS, Informix, NonStop SQL, Sybase, Teradata, Oracle (Rdb), Digital RMS, VSAM
notes: Comprehensive mainframe-to-relational source database support demonstrating enterprise breadth

metric_value: DB2 Parallel Edition, Informix, Microsoft SQL Server, NonStop SQL, Oracle (Rdb), Red Brick Warehouse, Sybase/SQL Server, Teradata
notes: Target RDBMS support showing multi-platform enterprise DW capability

metric_value: HP Intelligent Warehouse exchanges metadata with Prism via CDIF standard
notes: Strategic integration: HP Intelligent Warehouse as metadata exchange partner

metric_value: PLATINUM PR/MVS and R&O Rochade repositories exchange metadata via CDIF
notes: PLATINUM technology repositories as metadata integration partners

metric_value: Logic Works ERwin exports metadata to Prism Warehouse Directory
notes: ERwin as primary CASE tool integration; ERwin data modeling became industry standard

metric_value: Prism will integrate with CORBA to distribute and synchronize metadata
notes: Aberdeen: 'throughout 1997 Prism will evolve...toward CORBA and DCOM for distributing metadata'

metric_value: CORBA superseded by web services; Prism absorbed before CORBA integration matured
notes: CORBA declined to niche status by mid-2000s; Prism was acquired by Ardent in 1999 before full CORBA metadata architecture was delivered

metric_value: Metadata management will become critical infrastructure for all enterprise DW
notes: Aberdeen's metadata thesis as prediction: metadata will be THE key to warehouse scalability

metric_name: Metadata management as DW foundation - outcome
metric_value: Confirmed: data catalogs (Collibra Alation) became multi-billion dollar market; metadata governance foundational
notes: Modern data governance and data catalog market ($2B+ by 2020) validates Aberdeen's 1997 metadata thesis directly (Collibra valued at $3B+ in 2021)

metric_value: Prism positioned as competitive advantage toolset with strong customer base
notes: Aberdeen implies Prism is a long-term viable vendor with strong positioning

metric_value: Prism stock crashed from $35 to $2; acquired by Ardent for $42M (fire sale) in Jan 1999
notes: Despite Aberdeen's endorsement, Prism's stock fell dramatically; acquired by Ardent Software for $42M in January 1999 (ESJ article Jan 6 1999)

metric_value: More enterprises will demand systematic DW approach as warehouses scale in importance
notes: Aberdeen predicts accelerating DW adoption and increasing strategic importance

metric_value: Confirmed: DW became universal enterprise infrastructure; cloud DW (Snowflake Redshift BigQuery) emerged 2010s
notes: Data warehouse and BI became multi-hundred-billion dollar industry; cloud DW platforms like Snowflake (IPO 2020 at $33B) validate Aberdeen's DW importance prediction

metric_value: Five tracks (analysis design construction testing iteration/maintenance) with ~30 modules
notes: Prism Iterations: iterative data warehouse implementation methodology; precursor to modern agile DW practices

metric_value: Unix/NT data marts; Unix-based departmental marts/warehouses; full enterprise warehouses
notes: Three-tier packaging model matching Prism Scaleable Data Warehousing Solutions to deployment size

metric_value: Developers ought to leverage content and labor used to create one data mart in others
notes: Aberdeen articulates data mart reuse principle — precursor to modern DW hub-and-spoke and shared dimension models

metric_value: Short iterative steps to create and continuously fine-tune DW information architectures
notes: Aberdeen endorses iterative DW development — aligns with agile DW and Kimball's dimensional modeling methodology

metric_value: $55M projected 1998 revenue with 290 employees
notes: From Ardent acquisition announcement (ESJ Jan 1999): Prism projected $55M for 1998 revenue with 290 employees
